name: ZFS Manager
description: ZFS pool, dataset, and snapshot management for homelab servers
author: Vigil Add-ons

fields.label: Pool Name
fields.hint: Letters, digits, underscore, hyphen. Cannot start with a digit or match a zpool keyword (mirror, raidz, log, cache, etc.).

fields.label: Redundancy

options.label: Mirror — 2+ drives, highest durability, lose 50% capacity
Stripe — NO redundancy (data is lost on ANY device failure)

fields.hint: Pick the shape of the primary data vdev. Mirrors are easier to expand later; raidz is more space-efficient.

fields.label: Data Devices
fields.hint: Only disks not already in a pool are shown. Use all drives of the same size for best results.

fields.label: Log (SLOG) Devices
fields.hint: Dedicated low-latency device for synchronous writes. Leave empty unless you know you need it.

fields.label: SLOG Layout
fields.hint: Only used when at least one SLOG device is selected.

fields.label: Cache (L2ARC) Devices
fields.hint: Reads accelerate on SSDs. Always striped — no redundancy needed since L2ARC data is regenerated from disk on loss.

fields.label: Hot Spares
fields.hint: Spare drives that auto-replace on failure when autoreplace=on.

fields.label: ashift (sector size)

options.label: 12 (4K sectors — default, correct for most drives)

fields.hint: Cannot be changed after pool creation. When in doubt, leave at 12.

fields.label: Compression
fields.hint: Applied to new data.

options.label: Off (recommended — avoids a metadata write on every read)

fields.label: Mountpoint
fields.hint: Absolute path. Overrides the default /pool-name mountpoint.

fields.label: Force create (overwrite foreign labels)
fields.hint: RED TIER — reuses disks even when they carry existing zfs/lvm/md labels. Only enable if you have verified every selected disk is safe to overwrite.

fields.label: Type the pool name to confirm (force only)
fields.hint: Required only when Force is checked. Ignored otherwise.

fields.label: Auto TRIM
fields.hint: Background TRIM on SSDs. Off by default; recommend on for all-SSD pools.

fields.label: Auto Expand
fields.hint: Grow pool when all devices in a vdev are replaced with larger ones.

fields.label: Auto Replace
fields.hint: Initiate replace when a hot spare kicks in. Requires configured spares.

fields.label: Fail Mode

options.label: Wait (default — block I/O until device returns)

fields.hint: RED TIER — 'panic' will force a host reboot on the first pool I/O error.

fields.label: Delegation
fields.hint: Whether 'zfs allow' delegations are honored.

fields.label: Comment
fields.placeholder: Free-form note, shown in zpool list -o comment

confirm.title: Pause Scrub
confirm.message: Pause the running scrub on pool {row.name}?

confirm.title: Resume Scrub
confirm.message: Resume the paused scrub on pool {row.name}?

confirm.title: Cancel Scrub
confirm.message: Cancel the running scrub on pool {row.name}?

fields.label: Force (tear down active I/O)
fields.hint: Required when background processes still hold the pool open. Running VMs or containers must be stopped first to avoid data loss.

fields.label: Type the pool name to confirm
fields.hint: RED TIER — exporting the pool unmounts every dataset. The pool stays on disk but disappears from this host until re-imported.

config.empty_message: No importable pools detected. Pools that have been cleanly exported or were created on another host will appear here.

fields.label: Import under a different name (optional)
fields.placeholder: Leave empty to keep the original name
fields.hint: Useful when the pool name collides with an already-imported pool.

fields.label: Alternate root (optional)
fields.hint: Mount the pool under a temporary root instead of honoring its normal mountpoints. Leave empty for standard import.

fields.label: Import read-only
fields.hint: Safer for inspection. The pool is not writable until re-imported without this option.

fields.label: Force import (pool in use by another host)
fields.hint: RED TIER — force-importing a pool that is currently in use by another host corrupts it. Never use this in a cluster where the other host is still alive.

fields.label: Type the pool name to confirm (force only)
fields.hint: Required only when Force is checked. Ignored otherwise.

fields.label: Record Size
fields.hint: Changing record size only affects new data, not existing blocks

fields.label: Extended Attributes
fields.hint: 'sa' is the modern default; 'on' emulates the legacy format.

fields.label: Log Bias
fields.hint: Only meaningful if this pool has a SLOG device.

fields.label: Deduplication
fields.hint: RED TIER — dedup table lives in RAM (~320B/block). Effectively permanent: deduped blocks stay deduped even after dedup=off.

fields.label: Quota
fields.hint: Maximum space this dataset can consume. Use 'none' to remove.

fields.label: Reservation
fields.hint: Guaranteed minimum space for this dataset. Use 'none' to remove.

fields.label: Snapshot Name
fields.hint: Leave blank for auto-generated timestamp name

fields.label: Recursive
fields.hint: Also snapshot all child datasets

confirm.title: Delete Dataset
confirm.message: This will permanently destroy the dataset {row.name} and all its data. This action cannot be undone.
confirm.confirm_label: Type the full dataset name to confirm

fields.label: Dataset Name
fields.hint: Only letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, underscores. No spaces.

fields.label: Preset

options.label: General Purpose — good for most files and documents
Media Storage — optimized for large video/music files
VM/App Storage — balanced for virtual machines and containers
Database — tuned for databases that need data safety

fields.hint: Presets auto-configure record size, compression, atime, and sync

fields.label: Quota (optional)
fields.hint: Maximum space this dataset can consume

fields.label: Reservation (optional)
fields.hint: Guaranteed minimum space for this dataset

confirm.title: Delete {count} Snapshots
confirm.message: Permanently delete {count} snapshots? This cannot be undone.

confirm.title: Rollback to Snapshot
confirm.message: This will revert {row.dataset} to how it looked when snapshot {row.snap_name} was taken. Any data written after this snapshot will be permanently lost.
confirm.confirm_label: Type the dataset name to confirm

options.label: This and newer snapshots (destroys intermediate snapshots)
Everything after this (destroys all newer snapshots and bookmarks)

confirm.title: Delete Snapshot
confirm.message: Permanently delete snapshot {row.full_name}? This cannot be undone.
confirm.confirm_label: Type the full snapshot name to confirm

fields.label: Snapshot Name
fields.hint: Leave blank for auto-generated timestamp name (e.g. manual-2026-04-07-120000)

components.title: Pool Devices
components.description: Devices in your ZFS pools. Use row actions to manage individual devices.

confirm.title: Clear Error Counters
confirm.message: Reset all error counters (READ/WRITE/CKSUM) for pool {row.name}?

fields.label: Pool
fields.hint: The pool containing the device to replace

fields.label: Device to Replace
fields.hint: The failed or degraded device name as shown in pool status

fields.label: Replacement Device
fields.hint: Select an unused disk to replace the failed device

confirm.confirm_label: Type the pool name to confirm

fields.label: Vdev Type

options.label: RAIDZ1 — 3+ drives, survive 1 failure, lose 1 drive capacity
RAIDZ2 — 4+ drives, survive 2 failures, lose 2 drives capacity
RAIDZ3 — 5+ drives, survive 3 failures, lose 3 drives capacity

fields.hint: Should match the existing vdev type in your pool for best results

fields.label: Devices
fields.hint: Select the drives for the new vdev

confirm.confirm_label: Type the pool name to confirm expansion

fields.label: Device
fields.hint: The device name as shown in pool topology. This will reduce redundancy while offline.

fields.label: Device
fields.hint: Bring a previously offlined device back into the pool

form.title: Identify Drive Bay LED
form.description: Use ledctl to blink the drive bay LED so you can locate the physical drive in the chassis. Requires an SES-capable backplane and ledctl on the agent host.

fields.label: Device
fields.hint: The device name as shown in pool topology — short name or absolute path

options.label: Locate — turn the drive bay LED on
Off — return the drive bay LED to normal

components.title: Available Disks
components.description: Disks not currently used by any ZFS pool. These can be used for device replacement or pool expansion.

fields.label: Target
fields.hint: Dataset name (for snapshots) or pool name (for scrubs)

fields.label: Schedule
fields.hint: Standard 5-field cron expression (minute hour day month weekday)

fields.label: Naming Prefix
fields.hint: Snapshots will be named: prefix-2026-04-07-120000

fields.label: Retention (snapshots to keep)
fields.hint: 0 = unlimited. Older snapshots matching this prefix are automatically deleted.

confirm.title: Delete Scheduled Task
confirm.message: Delete the {row.task_type} task for {row.target}? Existing snapshots will not be removed.

fields.label: Schedule
fields.hint: Standard 5-field cron expression

fields.label: Naming Prefix
fields.hint: Snapshots named: prefix-2026-04-07-120000

fields.label: Retention
fields.hint: Older snapshots matching this prefix are automatically deleted

fields.label: Schedule
fields.hint: Standard 5-field cron expression

confirm.title: Cleanup Old Snapshots
confirm.message: Delete all snapshots older than the specified number of days for dataset {row.dataset}.
confirm.confirm_label: Type the dataset name to confirm

extra_fields.label: Delete snapshots older than (days)
extra_fields.hint: All snapshots for this dataset created more than this many days ago will be deleted

components.title: Replication Tasks
components.description: Periodic zfs send | zfs receive between two datasets on this host. Destinations are receive-owned — do not point them at a live dataset (zfs receive -F will roll back conflicting state).

confirm.title: Run Replication Now
confirm.message: Trigger a manual replication from {row.target} to {row.dest_target}? A fresh source snapshot will be taken before the send.

fields.label: Destination Dataset
fields.hint: Must be different from the source

fields.label: Snapshot Prefix
fields.hint: Source snapshots are named: prefix-2026-04-07-120000

fields.label: Retention
fields.hint: Applied on both source and destination after a successful run

fields.label: Bandwidth Limit (kB/s) — remote only
fields.hint: 0 or blank = unlimited. Ignored for local replication.

fields.label: Manage Retention on Remote (remote mode only)
fields.hint: Destroys old snapshots on the remote host after each successful receive, keeping the same count as source retention. Requires 'destroy,mount' zfs permissions for the SSH user on the destination dataset.

fields.label: Use Bookmarks for Incrementals (remote mode only)
fields.hint: Creates a zfs bookmark of each sent snapshot so source retention can prune aggressively without breaking the incremental chain.

result_view.title: Public Key — {row.ssh_key_name}
result_view.description: Append this single line to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on the destination host's SSH user so the agent can connect.

confirm.title: Rotate SSH Key — {row.ssh_key_name}
confirm.message: Rotating deletes the existing keypair and generates a new Ed25519 one. Until you copy the new public line into ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on every host that currently trusts this key, scheduled replications using it will fail with permission denied.

result_view.title: New Public Key — {row.ssh_key_name}
result_view.description: Old key is gone. Copy this line into ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on every host that needs to trust this agent.

confirm.title: Delete Replication Task
confirm.message: Delete the replication task from {row.target} to {row.dest_target}? Existing snapshots on source and destination will not be removed.

form.title: Create Local Replication Task
form.description: Replicate snapshots between two datasets on the same host with zfs send | zfs receive. First run is a full send; subsequent runs are incremental based on the most recent common snapshot.

fields.label: Source Dataset
fields.hint: The dataset whose snapshots will be sent

fields.label: Destination Dataset
fields.hint: The dataset that will receive the stream. Must already exist and must be different from the source. Use a dedicated receive-only dataset — zfs receive -F will roll back conflicting snapshots.

fields.label: Snapshot Prefix
fields.hint: Source snapshots will be named: prefix-2026-04-07-120000

fields.label: Retention
fields.hint: Applied on both source and destination after a successful run

form.title: Create Remote Replication Task
form.description: Send snapshots over SSH to a remote host's `zfs receive`. The agent auto-generates an Ed25519 keypair; copy its public line into ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on the remote user, then use Test Connection to pin the host key and verify access. First run is a full send; subsequent runs are incremental.

fields.label: Source Dataset
fields.hint: The local dataset whose snapshots will be sent

fields.label: Snapshot Prefix
fields.hint: Source snapshots will be named: prefix-2026-04-07-120000

fields.label: Remote Destination Dataset
fields.hint: Full ZFS path on the remote host, e.g. pool/dataset. Must already exist — use a dedicated receive-only dataset (zfs receive -F will roll back conflicts).

fields.label: SSH Port
fields.hint: Leave blank for the default 22

fields.label: Remote User
fields.hint: SSH user on the remote host. Needs `zfs receive` permission — grant via `zfs allow -u <user> receive,create,mount <dataset>`.

fields.label: SSH Key Name
fields.hint: Identifier (letters, digits, '-', '_') for the Ed25519 keypair. Fetch the public key with `GET /api/replication/keys/<name>/public` after creation and append it to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on the remote user.

fields.label: Source Retention
fields.hint: Applied on the source after a successful run. Also applied on the remote destination when 'Manage Retention on Remote' is enabled below.

fields.label: Manage Retention on Remote
fields.hint: Destroys old snapshots on the remote host after each successful receive, keeping the same count as source retention. Requires 'destroy,mount' zfs permissions for the SSH user on the destination dataset.

fields.label: Bandwidth Limit (kB/s)
fields.hint: 0 or blank = unlimited. Requires `pv` installed on the agent host.

fields.label: Use Bookmarks for Incrementals
fields.hint: Creates a zfs bookmark of each sent snapshot. Future incrementals use the bookmark as their base so aggressive source retention cannot break the incremental chain.

confirm.title: Delete Agent
confirm.message: Remove agent {row.agent_id} ({row.hostname}) from the registry? This only drops the manager-side record — the agent process will re-register itself on its next heartbeat unless you also stop it.

VIGIL_ZFS_AGENT_ADVERTISE_ADDR.label: Advertise Address
VIGIL_ZFS_AGENT_ADVERTISE_ADDR.hint: URL where the Manager can reach this agent

linux.label: Standard Linux
linux.hint: Deploy on any Linux host with ZFS installed. The agent mounts host ZFS binaries — use the Debian image for Proxmox/Ubuntu/Debian hosts.

components.title: I/O — Pools, Vdevs & Disks
components.description: Per-second read/write rates derived from zpool iostat. Rates are blank on the first frame after agent start; refresh once more to see them.
